_meta.purpose: Complete context tracking for all translatable strings
_meta.format: Each key has: context (where/how it's used), file (where in code), page (UI location), component (UI element), meaning (what it means to users), notes (translation notes), status

status_values.pending: Context not yet verified - needs research
status_values.skip: Should not be translated (brand names, technical terms, or intentionally English)

and all its records, wallets and recurr.key: and all its records, wallets and recurrent patterns?

1 ${_selectedCurrency ?? '[currency]'} =.meaning: Input field for entering conversion ratio (e.g., '1 EUR = [amount] USD')
1 ${_selectedCurrency ?? '[currency]'} =.notes: Universal/technical term - keep in English across all locales

Add expense.context: Quick action shortcut label for adding a new expense record directly from the device home screen
Add expense.meaning: A quick action button on the device home screen that opens the app to add a new expense
Add expense.notes: Short text for a home screen quick action icon. Keep concise.

Add income.context: Quick action shortcut label for adding a new income record directly from the device home screen
Add income.meaning: A quick action button on the device home screen that opens the app to add a new income
Add income.notes: Short text for a home screen quick action icon. Keep concise.

Add space between symbol and amount.key: Add space between symbol and amount

Additional translations contributed via.context: Contributors page footnote below the translators list

Adjust the overall keyboard scale.meaning: Describes that the dropdown controls the overall size/scale of the in-app numeric keyboard

Amount, Category, and Date/Time columns.notes: Keep technical terms Amount, Category, Date/Time consistent with field labels

Apply.meaning: Confirm and apply sorting preference change
Apply.notes: User clicks Apply to save their sort choice, not apply immediately

Auto.notes: Same word used generically for 'automatic' behaviour; keep short

Background color.context: Dropdown title for choosing the in-app keyboard's background color
Background color.meaning: Label for the setting that controls the background color behind the in-app numeric keyboard

Backup.context: Settings menu item for backup functionality

Black.context: Color name option shared by the keyboard background, button, and text color dropdowns

Black.meaning: The color black, used as a fixed color choice for keyboard customization

Button color.context: Dropdown title for choosing the in-app keyboard's button color
Button color.meaning: Label for the setting that controls the fill color of the keyboard's number/action buttons

Button text color.context: Dropdown title for choosing the in-app keyboard's button text/label color
Button text color.meaning: Label for the setting that controls the color of the digits/symbols printed on the keyboard buttons

CSV Preview.context: Section header showing a preview of parsed CSV data before mapping
CSV Preview.meaning: Preview of CSV file contents (first few rows in a table)

Unable to create a backup: please, delet.key: Unable to create a backup: please, delete manually the old backup

Unique categories.context: Summary row showing the number of distinct categories found in the CSV
Unique categories.page: Settings > Import from CSV > Import Summary
Unique categories.meaning: How many different category names appear in the CSV data

Unique tags.context: Summary row showing the number of distinct tags found in the CSV
Unique tags.page: Settings > Import from CSV > Import Summary
Unique tags.meaning: How many different tag names appear in the CSV data

Unique wallets.context: Summary row showing the number of distinct wallet names found in the CSV
Unique wallets.page: Settings > Import from CSV > Import Summary
Unique wallets.meaning: How many different wallet names appear in the CSV data
Unique wallets.notes: Standard statistical term. Shown alongside Unique categories and Unique tags.

Unparseable rows.context: Summary row showing how many CSV rows could not be parsed
Unparseable rows.page: Settings > Import from CSV > Import Summary
Unparseable rows.meaning: Rows that failed to parse and will be skipped
Unparseable rows.notes: Keep concise. Shown with a warning/error color.

Use Category Colors in Pie Chart.key: Use Category Colors in Pie Chart

Use global default (%s).context: Decimal digits dropdown option on the Add/Edit Currency page
Use global default (%s).component: DropdownMenuItem label in the per-currency decimal digits dropdown
Use global default (%s).meaning: Option meaning 'use the global decimal digits setting' instead of a per-currency override. %s is replaced with the current global digit count (e.g. 'Use global default (2)').
Use global default (%s).notes: Keep the %s placeholder intact; it is filled with a number via .fill().

Visit our support page with ways to cont.key: Visit our support page with ways to contribute
Visit our support page with ways to cont.context: Card subtitle on the support page for the web support option
Visit our support page with ways to cont.meaning: Describes that the web page has both help information and contribution options

Visualise tags in the main page.key: Visualise tags in the main page

Visualise wallet name in the main page.key: Visualise wallet name in the main page

Warnings.context: Section header for warning messages on the import summary screen
Warnings.page: Settings > Import from CSV > Import Summary
Warnings.meaning: Header for a list of potential issues found during CSV parsing

What should happen to the records in thi.key: What should happen to the records in this wallet? Transfer records involving this wallet will always be deleted.

When deleting a record, add back its amo.key: When deleting a record, add back its amount to the wallet balance

White.context: Color name option shared by the keyboard background, button, and text color dropdowns
White.meaning: The color white, used as a fixed color choice for keyboard customization

With space.meaning: Add space between symbol and amount (e.g., $ 100)

Without space.meaning: No space between symbol and amount (e.g., $100)

Would you like to add your own currency?.key: Would you like to add your own currency?

Write us directly — we read every messag.key: Write us directly — we read every message
Write us directly — we read every messag.context: Card subtitle on the support page for the email option
Write us directly — we read every messag.meaning: Encourages users to email, assuring them their feedback is valued
Write us directly — we read every messag.notes: Friendly tone, keep the em dash

Yes.meaning: User confirmation/positive response to confirmation prompt
Yes.notes: Paired with 'No' button. Often kept as-is in target languages.

You have wallets with different currenci.key: You have wallets with different currencies. Set up conversion rates.

You need to set a category first. Go to.key: You need to set a category first. Go to Category tab and add a new category.

Your main currency is the reference for.key: Your main currency is the reference for all conversion rates. Pick the currency you use most often.

apostrophe.meaning: The apostrophe character (') used in strings
apostrophe.notes: Universal/technical term - keep in English across all locales

underscore.meaning: The underscore character (_) used in strings
underscore.notes: Used for customizing string separators - translate as the actual character name

Custom.context: Repeat-period dropdown option letting the user define an arbitrary recurrence interval (e.g. every 6 months) instead of picking one of the fixed presets (day/week/month/year).
Custom.page: Edit record / Edit recurrent pattern > Repeat field
Custom.component: DropdownMenuItem label in the repeat-interval dropdown
Custom.meaning: Selects the custom-interval mode: user enters a number and picks a unit (days/weeks/months/years).
Custom.notes: Same generic word as elsewhere in the app; here it specifically means 'user-defined recurrence interval'.

Every %d %s.context: Template for the human-readable label of a custom recurrence interval, e.g. 'Every 6 Months'. %d is the numeric interval value, %s is the pluralized unit noun (Days/Weeks/Months/Years).
Every %d %s.page: Recurrent Records list (group header) and Edit record > Repeat field
Every %d %s.meaning: Describes how often a custom recurring pattern repeats.
Every %d %s.notes: Word order may not translate literally in every language (the unit noun is inserted after the count); keep the two placeholders in the same relative order if possible, or reorder as your language's grammar requires.

Days.context: Unit option in the custom-interval unit dropdown, always shown in plural form regardless of the entered number (matches the app's existing simple i18n pattern, no singular/plural agreement).
Days.page: Edit record / Edit recurrent pattern > Repeat field > Custom interval unit dropdown
Days.meaning: The custom recurrence interval is measured in days.
Days.notes: Always plural, used together with a numeric field, e.g. '6' + 'Days'.

Weeks.context: Unit option in the custom-interval unit dropdown, always shown in plural form regardless of the entered number.
Weeks.page: Edit record / Edit recurrent pattern > Repeat field > Custom interval unit dropdown
Weeks.meaning: The custom recurrence interval is measured in weeks.
Weeks.notes: Always plural, used together with a numeric field, e.g. '3' + 'Weeks'.

Months.context: Unit option in the custom-interval unit dropdown, always shown in plural form regardless of the entered number.
Months.page: Edit record / Edit recurrent pattern > Repeat field > Custom interval unit dropdown
Months.meaning: The custom recurrence interval is measured in months.
Months.notes: Always plural, used together with a numeric field, e.g. '6' + 'Months' (the motivating use case: a bi-annual payment).

Years.context: Unit option in the custom-interval unit dropdown, always shown in plural form regardless of the entered number.
Years.page: Edit record / Edit recurrent pattern > Repeat field > Custom interval unit dropdown
Years.meaning: The custom recurrence interval is measured in years.
Years.notes: Always plural, used together with a numeric field, e.g. '2' + 'Years'.

Enter a valid number.context: Form validation error shown under the custom-interval numeric field when the entered value is empty, non-numeric, or not a positive integer.
Enter a valid number.page: Edit record / Edit recurrent pattern > Repeat field > Custom interval value field
Enter a valid number.meaning: Tells the user the interval count they entered is invalid and must be a positive whole number.
Enter a valid number.notes: Generic validation message; keep concise, no specific number format needed.
